_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to recognise and use our personal strengths to help ourselves and others, and how to develop interests that help us grow.

_source.w: We are learning how to recognise and use our personal strengths to help ourselves and others, and how to develop interests that help us grow.
y: Knowing our strengths gives us confidence to try new things and helps us support the people around us.
Developing personal interests makes life more enjoyable and can lead to discovering hidden talents.
Understanding what we are good at helps us know how to contribute when working with others.
eg: We list our personal strengths — like being a good listener, being brave, or being creative — and share examples of when we have used them.
We think of a time we helped someone by using one of our strengths, like cheering up a friend because we are funny.
We choose a personal interest we want to develop further and explain why it matters to us.
We interview a classmate about their strengths and interests and present what we learned to the class.
We draw a 'strength shield' with pictures of the things we are good at and display them on the wall.
We set a small goal related to an interest — like learning a new song or reading a new book — and track our progress.
